name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-77
title: rabbit-config.py opens 'permissions':{} via setdefault then write_json — write_json runs even for no-op listing/list path?
description: Actually verifying: cmd_allowed_tools and cmd_bash_allow list paths (action=='') do NOT call perm_allow (they iterate cfg.get(...).get(...,[])). Only the add/remove paths call perm_allow. add path always writes when entry is new. remove path writes only if entry was found. The 'Not present' / 'Already present' branches do NOT write — confirmed. False alarm; not actually a bug. RECLASSIFY: instead — actual concern is that 'allowed-tools' list output does not preserve the input's setting order or use the spec's 'in array order' wording explicitly. settings.local.json deserialization preserves insertion order in Python 3.7+ which is fine. Minor: SKILL.md description claims subcommands act on settings.local.json but the CLI Surface table in body just says 'in settings.local.json' without explaining the source/destination split documented in spec.md (Inv 50). New users invoking from SKILL.md without reading spec may try to add team-wide entries via allowed-tools (wrong file).

history.note: wave-4 cleanup; fixed in e10610a (impl) and 91f2c68 (tests)
